p.strong: Why Identity Mapping Is Required Before Enabling Paging

div.p: Condition: V == P The physical page containing code is mapped to its own numerical value.

g.text: Target: Root of Page Table Hierarchy
Paging logic is primed but not yet active.

div.p: ⚠️ T+0 Micro-operation: MMU immediately starts interpreting EIP as a Virtual Address.

g.text: Identity mapping prevents a fetch discontinuity.

ol.li: CPU looks up IDT for #PF handler
IDT fetch fails (V != P for IDT)

div.p: The code that enables paging must exist in a region where Virtual Address == Physical Address. This "Identity Map" or "1:1 Map" is the bridge that allows the instruction pointer to remain valid across the cycle where address translation is activated.

text.tspan: // Assume page_dir is already prepared with identity mappings
// 3. Absolute jump to ensure EIP is fully synchronized
// Assume page_dir is already prepared with identity mappings
// 3. Absolute jump to ensure EIP is fully synchronized
